name: Adore Me
heading: Adore Me Reviews
subheading: Is Adore Me Right For You?
content:
What It’s Like to Subscribe to Adore Me

 

Read reviews further down the page, too, for more personal experiences.

How the Adore Me Elite Box Works

When you subscribe, the first thing you do is take a style quiz to let their stylists know what kinds of items you’re looking for and what your style preferences are.

Your box is then curated and shipped for free, with a $20 styling fee. You have seven days to try on your items and send back anything you aren’t loving with a prepaid shipping label. The $20 is directly applied as a discount towards any items you keep! Price Range: Each box includes 3-5 matching sets (top and bottoms) that range from $39.95 to $59.95 per set.

Their stylists then take your selections and any feedback and curate better and better boxes each month with clothes you’re sure to adore.

Adore Me Pros and Cons

Pros

  • Lots of styles to choose from to find something that makes you feel confident and sexy
  • Able to try on in the comfort of your own home
  • Wide range of sizes
  • Stylist curations sure to surprise and delight

Cons

  • Some MSA users recently reported poor customer service experiences
  • Sizes may miss the mark in first couple deliveries
  • Some styles don’t feel like the right quality for the price

What Else You Should Know About Adore Me

 

Not wanting to commit to a subscription just yet? Or maybe you already know what styles you like and don’t need a stylist’s curation? You can craft your own boxes by shopping at their store and ordering 1 to 4 sets directly.

Is Adore Me Worth It?

 

If you’re looking to explore new styles of intimates, swimsuits, or lingerie recommended by expert stylists, Adore Me is a great option. The ability to try these on at home is a less awkward experience and helps you decide whether they’re really right for you.

If you generally prefer more toned-down styles, just need more basic daily wear, or are looking for a more affordable option, then Adore Me might not be right for you. If you’re new to subscriptions generally, then Adore Me might also be more difficult to work through customer service if something goes wrong with one of your deliveries.

How Adore Me Compares to Other Top Intimates Subscriptions

 

Adore Me VS Wantable Sleep & Body Edits: Each of these subscriptions has a $20 styling fee that is applied towards the cost of any items you keep, with free returns on anything you don’t keep. Wantable ships you 7 individual items which are priced individually and are usually very affordable, compared to Adore Me’s 3-5 full sets priced at $39 or higher. Wantable generally has more of a focus on comfort and daily wear, while Adore Me has bolder and sexier options.

 

Adore Me VS Savage X Fenty: Adore Me is a curated subscription where sets are sent to you every month and you buy what you keep, compared to Savage X Fenty which offers a VIP membership with various benefits and exclusive deals, but is otherwise just a recurring charge for $49.95 which gives you store credit. SXF also offers monthly curations by Rihanna or another celebrity guest for a discount, but does not ship you anything you don’t order yourself. SXF has a much wider range of selections for both men and women, ranging from casual to bold.
description:
Adore Me is an online clothing brand offering intimates including bras, panties, sleepwear, swimwear, loungewear, and lingerie. Adore Me Elite brings their most popular products right to your door each month for a $20 styling fee, which is applied directly towards any items you keep.

Adore Me offers a wide selection of options when it comes to intimates, including bralettes, push-up, and padded bra styles, in both sexy and everyday designs. Adore Me bra sizes run from 30A to 46DDD, and panty sets come in sizes ranging from XS to 4XL. High quality sleepwear, athletic sets, swimwear, and ready-to-wear styles are also available.

Adore me is the most reviewed bra subscription in our Directory, and the second most popular lingerie subscription too!
title: Adore Me Reviews: Everything You Need To Know

name: Book of the Month
heading: Book of the Month Reviews
subheading: Is Book of the Month Right For You?
description: Book of the Month is a popular online subscription service for books that helps millennial women discover the best new reads. We announce a curated selection of between 5 - 7 new and early release hardcover books every month. Members choose one on the site, and we ship it to them in a bright blue box. We offer the best price for new release hardcover fiction and new members can get their first month for 9.99. Our membership is risk-free, meaning members can skip any month or any time.

name: Bookroo
heading: Bookroo Reviews
subheading: Is Bookroo Right For You?
description:
Remarkable Books. Charming Deliveries. Lifelong Readers.

Bookroo is a subscription for your kids that helps them fall in love with reading by combining the excitement of opening a gift with the discovery of new treasured books. We send hidden gems your kids will LOVE, so you can build a home library of quality books stress-free! One of Parent Magazine's Top 10 Subscription Services 2017! Bookroo offers a Board Book Subscription (3 board books per month, ages 0-3), a Picture Book Subscription (2 hardcover picture books per month, ages 2-6) and a Chapter Book Subscription (2 chapter books per month, lisitng here, ages 7-10).

 

What you can expect with Bookroo:

1. One-of-a-kind book selection platform. We created the world’s largest platform dedicated entirely to children's books to discover and share amazing books by talented authors and illustrators that will become treasured favorites.
2. Unbox a premium reading experience. Our books arrive in a bright, colorful box and each book is hand wrapped like a gift.
3. Raise lifelong readers. The excitement kids feel for their Bookroo deliveries develops a lifelong love of books and reading.
4. Save money. Not only are you getting a premium unboxing experience and discovering new favorite books, but the retail value of the books in our boxes exceeds the subscription cost.
5. Purchase with confidence. We provide a 100% Money Back Guarantee. If you’re not happy for any reason with your first box, we’ll refund your money.

name: IPSY Extra
heading: IPSY Extra Reviews
subheading: Is IPSY Extra Right For You?
description:
IPSY Extra ($32 per month) is one of the most popular beauty subscription boxes that we review, with lots of loyal customers. They promise 5 full-size beauty products each month from a variety of both indie and established brands, with a total retail value of up to $200 in every box. The beauty items featured vary from month to month, and will include beauty tools as well as skincare and haircare items—but IPSY Extra definitely leans more makeup-heavy than other popular beauty boxes. 

Key Info

PRICE: $32 Monthly

FOUNDED IN: 2013

SHIPS TO: US and Canada (additional $6.95)

RETAIL VALUE: up to $200.00

name: Cate & Chloe VIP
heading: Cate & Chloe VIP Reviews
subheading: Is Cate & Chloe VIP Right For You?
description: Cate & Chloe VIP is a monthly jewelry subscription. Each month you get up to $200 worth of Cate & Chloe jewelry. Subscriptions range from $19.99 for one piece of jewelry, $29.99 for two, and $39.99 for three.

name: Alltrue
heading: Alltrue Reviews
subheading: Is Alltrue Right For You?
description: Alltrue (formerly CAUSEBOX) is a seasonal lifestyle membership that unites a community of individuals who stand for good, seek truth, and take action. Every season, Alltrue members receive a curation of carefully vetted socially-conscious lifestyle products (from fashion and homewares to skincare and jewelry). Every product is sustainable and ethical, so you can treat yourself and know that you're making an impact. Additionally, Alltrue members get access to a 24/7 Members-Only Essentials Shop, where they'll find the best prices on sustainable alternatives to everyday products, from shampoo to shaving cream. Members have access to Alltrue's curated seasonal pop-up markets, which feature more than 700+ products at up to 70% off. Finally, Alltrue members get exclusive access to premium content in Alltrue's seasonal print magazine All Good Things, and you join a community of several hundred thousand women who are connected by positive, shared values as highlighted in Alltrue's podcast, Meet Us Here.

name: Craft Beer Club
heading: Craft Beer Club Reviews
subheading: Is Craft Beer Club Right For You?
description: The Craft Beer Club discovers exceptional craft brews from around the country and delivers them each month direct-to-you or your gift recipient. Every selection is produced by small-production, independent brewers who use only traditional brewing ingredients and time-honored brewing methods.

name: Curology
heading: Curology Reviews
subheading: Is Curology Right For You?
description: Curology is a health and wellness subscription that offers personalized skincare formulas prescribed by medical providers. No matter your skin type, Curology will work with you to understand your skin’s history and condition, and provide a powerful formulation tailored to your specific needs. Curology also has a full staff of dermatology providers including board-certified physicians, physician assistants, and nurse practitioners, and subscribers are partnered with someone who is licensed in their home state. You’ll work with the same provider throughout your Curology journey, and they can answer your questions, help tweak your formula if needed, and more.

name: FabFitFun
heading: FabFitFun Reviews
subheading: Is FabFitFun Right For You?
description: "FabFitFun is a seasonal lifestyle subscription that sends subscribers 6-8 items every season, worth up to $300 in value. Boxes include amazing products that are trend-savvy ranging from full-size skincare and beauty products, to wellness products, to accessories, to decor and more every season. While some items are picked by the FabFitFun team, every season you'll get to pick out 4-8 items yourself. You can make the most of your FabFitFun subscription membership by shopping year-round flash sales with deep discounts or seasonal add-ons to get even more in every box."

name: Glamour Jewelry Box
heading: Glamour Jewelry Box Reviews
subheading: Is Glamour Jewelry Box Right For You?
description: Glamour Jewelry Box is an award-winning monthly jewelry subscription service dedicated to helping women discover new jewelry pieces in an affordable and convenient way. Each month members will receive curated jewelry pieces including necklaces, bracelets, and earrings. Glamour Jewelry Box does all the work for you.

name: HANGSQUAD earrings
heading: HANGSQUAD earrings Reviews
subheading: Is HANGSQUAD earrings Right For You?
description:
HANGSQUAD earrings offers a seasonal subscription box (of just earrings!) curated for the season. 
 
 On-trend earrings shipped directly to your mailbox. Choose from our 5, 10, or 20-pack. Choose your style, sizing, and other preference on your STYLE PROFILE. Our stylists choose the earrings in your box according to your style profile. 
 
 Purchase our seasonal boxes as a one-time order or set up your quarterly seasonal subscription (4 boxes/year).

name: Home Chef
heading: Home Chef Reviews
subheading: Is Home Chef Right For You?
content:
What It’s Like to Subscribe to Home Chef

 

Read reviews further down the page, too, for more personal experiences.

How Home Chef Works

There are 3 main steps to using Home Chef. First, you will need to provide some initial information: how many meals do you want to order, and what are your cooking and diet preferences to see the most relevant recommendations. Then, you can customize your meals by swapping out ingredients or by adding portions. While Home Chef recommends what it thinks you might like, you can still see and order from the full menu. Finally, you’ll receive your ready-to-cook ingredients along with step-by-step instructions on how to make your meals.

Home Chef Recipes

  • Honey-Ginger Chicken Katsu
  • Pork Chop and Red Wine Poached Figs
  • Pumpkin Cranberry Cake
  • Korean-Style Tofu Lettuce Cups
  • Seared Scallops with French Onion Risotto
  • Garlicky BLT Burger

Pricing Options

Pricing is based on how many people you are cooking for as well as how many recipes you want to receive.

Number of People (2,4, or 6) Recipes Per Week (2-6) Price Per Week (+Shipping for Orders Under $40) Monthly Total
2 2 $49.95 $199.80
2 3 $53.94 $215.76
2 4 $71.92 $287.68
2 5 $89.90 $359.60
2 6 $107.88 $431.52
4 2 $71.92 $287.68
4 3 $107.88 $431.52
4 4 $143.84 $575.36
4 5 $179.80 $719.20
4 6 $215.76 $863.04
6 2 $107.88 $431.52
6 3 $161.82 $647.28
6 4 $215.76 $863.04
6 5 $269.70 $1078.80
6 6 $323.64 $1294.56

Home Chef Pros and Cons

Pros

  • Boxes include a Home Chef cookbook that contains all of your recipe cards.
  • Makes sure to include ideas and instructions for substitutions
  • Offers both oven-ready prepared meals and meals that require more cooking

Cons

  • Extremely limited vegetarian and vegan meals.
  • Products are only organic on occasion,
  • Limited options for dietary preferences, like keto or vegan

What Diets Does Home Chef Accommodate?

Diet Is Home Chef Good For This Diet?
Keto Yes, there are some keto options
Gluten-Free Sort of, there are some gluten-free options but it’s hard to tell.
Vegetarian Yes, there are some vegetarian options.
Vegan No, not from what we can tell.
High Protein Yes, there are several options.
Low Carb Yes, there are several options.
Organic Not exactly. It depends on when you order, but it varies week by week.

What Else You Should Know About Home Chef

 

Home Chef is one of the few meal kit subscription services that actually allows its customers to choose the length of time they’re willing to put into the preparation and cooking of each meal. Many other similar subscriptions do not provide this option which makes Home Chef great for both people on the go as well as individuals that have the extra time to put in their own flare. It also offers one of the most affordable options we’ve seen for this type of meal subscription box.

Is Home Chef Worth It?

 

If you’re not overly concerned with strict ingredient sourcing or affected by certain dietary restrictions or allergies, Home Chef should be a fine choice. It’s certainly was less expensive than practically all other meal kit subscriptions and still has quite the variety of options for recipes. Home Chef is also among the few subscription services that actually provide recipe cards within your own cookbook to create an even more personalized experience that you can build upon.

How Home Chef Compares to Other Top Meal Subscriptions

 

Home Chef_ VS HelloFresh: HelloFresh is a more affordable option and starts at $7.49 per meal vs. $8.99 per meal. However, it seems to offer fewer customization options and does not include the ability to select meals based on the estimated time that’s required to cook them. Thus, if you are ok with trading personalization for cost, then Hello Fresh may be the better option for you.

Home Chef VS Blue Apron: Blue Apron also offers a similar subscription box where its customers are required to cook their meals from scratch. However, it also offers other products such as wine and kitchen accessories. Blue Apron is somewhat cheaper on average and its meals start at around $7.49. However, it only offers up to 4 recipes per week for either 2 or 4 individuals, making Home Chef the best option for larger families or individuals that want to try out more recipes each week.

Home Chef_ VS Freshly: Freshly offers prepared food delivery subscriptions that start at around the same cost at $9.00/serving. Deciding whether Home Chef or Freshly is best for you depends on how much time you’re willing to spend each week on cooking and whether or not you enjoy creative cooking. Home Chef is best for customers who want to cook from scratch and add their own style to meals, whereas Freshly is best for people who are on the go and have limited time available for cooking.
description:
Home Chef is a meal kit subscription service that allows customers to pick out specific meals each week that best fit their tastes and schedules. Whether you’re cooking for yourself and your spouse or having a full house of kids, Home Chef offers recipes that everyone can enjoy. Each Home Chef box contains pre-portioned ingredients along with recipe cards and your own recipe binder. There’s a weekly rotating menu that has plenty of meal options to choose from, such as calorie-conscious or vegetarian.

Home Chef also offers prepared meals under their Home Chef Fresh 'n' Easy brand which you can order alongside meal kits that you have to cook yourself. This flexibility gives you more options to adapt to your schedule and needs.

Home Chef was voted onto our Reader's Choice list of Best Meal Kit Subscription Boxes so be sure to check it out!

name: HUM Nutrition
heading: HUM Nutrition Reviews
subheading: Is HUM Nutrition Right For You?
description:
HUM was founded in 2012 by Walter Faulstroh, as a result of his personal struggle with his skin and breakouts. After years of trying topical products and prescription drugs that were only a temporary fix, Faulstroh knew there was a link between nutrition and his skin. He worked with top nutritionists and dietitians to conduct extensive research to formulate products with clean and certified ingredients.

HUM now offers a wide range of products, which address everything from gut health, women’s health, sleep, stress, and skin with the mission to enable people to feel good in their skin and body. HUM is backed by extensive, scientific studies and formulated by top Nutritionists. Each bottle is made with clinically tested ingredients and a clean certified formula, FREE of the ‘Tricky 12’ - common, bad-for-you ingredients such as artificial colors, sweeteners, flavors, and preservatives. HUM’s safe and effective proprietary blends help you feel confident in your wellness goals. 

The brand’s commitment to sustainability is just as important as its mission to help individuals look and feel great. Every bottle is fully recyclable and made from 100% Prevented Ocean Plastic™. To date, HUM has saved over 24M+ bottles from entering our oceans.

name: Hunt a Killer
heading: Hunt a Killer Reviews
subheading: Is Hunt a Killer Right For You?
description: "Hunt a Killer is a monthly interactive subscription box that transforms you into a detective hot on the trail of a serial killer. It's all the thrill of watching your favorite true crime show or doing an escape room delivered to your door! Each month, you'll receive police files, case files, autopsies, evidence, pictures, audio files, and more—items that will immerse you in the experience and which you'll have to interpret in order to solve the mystery. Play alone or with friends. Need help? Check out their community of over 100,000 active members."

name: Ipsy Original
heading: Ipsy Original Reviews
subheading: Is Ipsy Original Right For You?
content:
What It’s Like to Subscribe to Ipsy

 

Read reviews further down the page, too, for more personal experiences.

How Ipsy Works

Subscribing to Ipsy is easy - the sign up process starts with a quiz which helps them match you with products they think you’ll love.

You pay for your first bag upfront, but then each month you’ll be charged at the end of the month before your next month’s bag ships. (See plans and pricing below)

But it’s not all sitting and waiting - each month, you also get to choose 1 or more products depending on your plan. These choices are also customized to you and are only available while supplies last, so make your choices as early as possible!

Your delivery will come in Ipsy packaging, and all your products come in a usable new makeup makeup bag, too.

Once you’ve had a chance to try out your products, you can go in and rate what Ipsy sent you to help give feedback that they then use to improve your future bags - the more feedback you give, the better Ipsy gets at giving you products you’ll be delighted with.

If you’re not satisfied or you just don’t need any makeup for a while, you can skip or cancel your subscription via your profile and aren’t committed long term.

Ipsy Plans Pricing and Comparison

You subscribe to either the standard IPSY Original or upgrade to the IPSY Extra.

You also have the option to subscribe to IPSY Ultimate, which is a quarterly replacement for either of the other plans. For the IPSY Ultimate, Ipsy collaborates with a celeb to curate additional full-size products for you to pick from and boosts your bag.

Your Plan Ipsy Original Ipsy Extra Ipsy Ultimate
Frequency Monthly Monthly Quarterly Replacement
Price per Delivery $15 $32 $65
Products 5 Samples 5 Full-Size 8 full-size
Products You Pick One Three Three

Note: IPSY Ultimate quarterly replacement months are February, May, August, and November.

See full IPSY Extra reviews here.

See full IPSY Ultimate reviews here.

Ipsy Pros and Cons

Pros

  • Premium products with great brand selection variety
  • Personalized
  • Affordable
  • Complete makeup kit with makeup bag every month

Cons

  • Could be frustrating if product personalization misses the mark
  • If you miss choice time you could miss out on a product you might have wanted

What Else You Should Know About Ipsy

 

Get the most out of your Ipsy Subscription: Two factors play a big role in what products are sent to you each month: (1) the quiz you take when signing up, and (2) the feedback you give about the products you receive each month. The more information you share the better your products will be!

Update the quiz on your profile whenever your preferences change or if your products aren’t quite right, in addition to rating your products every month so Ipsy can deliver better products for you next month.

You can choose one product each month - Don’t forget! You do get the ability to customize your bag by making a choice each month, but supplies are limited. If you want to get your first choice, be sure to log in and choose as early as possible. We announce choice time each month so be sure to log in and follow ipsy on this page to get an alert! You can also see the deadline ahead of time by checking upcoming events on Ipsy’s site.

You can earn points to redeem for products. Referring friends to Ipsy can earn you points towards add-ons.

Is Ipsy Worth It?

 

After over 100 full box reviews and 1000 user ratings, we think Ipsy definitely ranks as one of the “must try” subscriptions if you’re looking to explore beauty subscriptions and try the latest trends. It’s hard to go wrong starting with the basic IPSY Original at only $15. If you like it and find yourself needing your products to last longer, then upgrading to IPSY Extra can bring full sizes. Or, if you only want to go big once a quarter, the IPSY Ultimate can get you more products in full sizes, plus stay on top of the latest trends with celeb curations.

How Ipsy Compares to Other Top Beauty Subscriptions

 

Ipsy VS Allure Beauty Box: Allure Beauty Box is most comparable to the IPSY Extra, priced at $25 compared to IPSY Extra at $32. You can get Allure down under $21 per bag if you commit to a whole year subscription. With Allure you receive five or more products each month, but only three are guaranteed full-size compared to IPSY Extras' five full-size products. With Allure your products are curated by editors of the magazine, but they are less personalized than with Ipsy.

Ipsy Original VS Birchbox: Birchbox costs $22 and is most comparable to the basic IPSY Original at $15. Both boxes send you five sample-sized products. Birchbox tends to deliver more well-known brands and also leans a little more into skincare and wellness products rather than strictly makeup and beauty. Birchbox was recently re-acquired and is trending more towards health and how your nutrition can affect your skin, so expect to see more of those types of products appear as optional add-ons or one-time boxes, if not in your regular Birchbox delivery.

 

There are many more beauty subscription brands, see the list of best beauty subscriptions for more details.
description:
IPSY Original is one of the most popular and affordable subscriptions around, and our readers have selected it as one of the top beauty boxes for the last 5 years running! IPSY Original connects members with five makeup, skincare, hair care, and other beauty samples each month, plus a cute makeup bag. IPSY is personalised to your individual profile when you take their onboarding quiz. 

Ipsy Plans Pricing and Comparison

You subscribe to either the IPSY Original or upgrade to IPSY Extra (formerly BoxyCharm by IPSY, beofre that, Glam Blag Plus and Boxycharm base).

You also have the option to subscribe to the IPSY Ultimate (formerly Icon Box, before that Glam Bag X), which is a quarterly replacement for either of the other plans. For IPSY Ultimate, Ipsy collaborates with a celeb to curate additional full-size products for you to pick from and boosts your bag.

Note: IPSY Ultimate quarterly replacement months are February, May, August, and November.

name: IPSY Ultimate
heading: IPSY Ultimate Reviews
subheading: Is IPSY Ultimate Right For You?
description:
IPSY Ultimate (previously known as Icon Box, prior to that IPSY Glam Bag X) is a quarterly, limited edition upgrade available for IPSY base subscribers. It includes 8 full-size products (subscribers choose 3!) worth up to $350, specially curated by the biggest icons and celebs in beauty. The assortment includes 5 Power Picks, which are superstar, must-try products or brands handpicked by IPSY's team of experts.

Every limited edition IPSY Ultimate is curated by a different celeb, and a new one drops every February, May, August, and November. During those months, IPSY Ultimate replaces subscribers' usual monthly IPSY Extra drop.

name: KiwiCo Kiwi Crate
heading: KiwiCo Kiwi Crate Reviews
subheading: Is KiwiCo Kiwi Crate Right For You?
content:
What It’s Like to Subscribe to KiwiCo

 

Read reviews further down the page, too, for more personal experiences.

How KiwiCo Crates Work

KiwiCo Crates are easy to get started with. KiwiCo crates cater to different age ranges and come in a variety of themes. Each box delivers a new project for you and your kid(s) to explore and learn something new.

To help you pick the right box, KiwiCo wants to know your child’s name and age. A nice feature that helps crates feel even more special to your child is that boxes are addressed to them and not to you.

From there, it’s up to you if you want to keep receiving boxes or if you want to skip or cancel anytime.

KiwiCo Crate Options and Pricing

Every KiwiCo Crate has a different theme targeted to different age ranges. You can also order boxes as monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, or annual subscriptions. Monthly subscriptions are the standard highest price. Annual subscriptions give you the largest discount, with quarterly and semi-annual subscriptions with prices in between.

Crate Line Age Range Theme Standard Price Lowest Annual Price Frequency
Panda Crate 0 - 2 years For Babies to Explore $20.95 $15.95 Bi-Monthly
Koala Crate 2 - 4 years For Toddlers to Explore $23.95 $18.50 Monthly
Kiwi Crate 5 - 8 years Science & Art $23.95 $18.50 Monthly
Atlas Crate 6 - 11 years Geography and Culture $23.95 $18.50 Monthly
Yummy Crate 6 - 14 years Culinary Science $23.95 $18.50 Monthly
Doodle Crate 9 - 16+ years Art & Craft $23.95 $18.50 Monthly
Tinker Crate 9 - 14 years Science and Engineering $23.95 $18.50 Monthly
Maker Crate 14 to adult Art & Design $31.95 $25.95 Monthly
Eureka Crate 12 to adult Engineering Design $31.95 $25.95 Monthly

Prices are per box.

KiwiCo Pros and Cons

Pros

  • Great educational projects to keep your kids occupied
  • Good for development at all ages
  • Monthly shipments means there’s always something new to keep them engaged
  • Crates contain everything you need, no need to go shopping

Cons

  • Could be messy
  • Might need some supervision so they don’t get lost or discouraged
  • Subscription will renew if not fully cancelled; be sure to cancel and not skip

What Else You Should Know About KiwiCo

 

These crates make for great gifts for kids. Based on their age and their interests, you can find the right crate and use KiwiCos awesome gifting options to deliver it how you want. You can manage the subscription yourself or buy them a gift card to choose on their own. If you want to manage the subscription yourself, you can have the first box shipped to yourself to wrap and give in person!

Is KiwiCo Worth It?

 

We’ve reviewed KiwiCo over 60 times so far with more to come, and we can confidently say that these crates are more than worth it for kids to get engaged with sciences, crafts, and critical thinking. Because every project is unique, some crates might be more fun for your kids than others, but on the whole the engagement with the sciences is worth it.

How KiwiCo Compares to Other Top Subscriptions for Kids

 

KiwiCo VS MEL Science: MEL Science has four crates that are more directly STEM related: A STEM kids box, Chemistry, Physics, and Medicine. The MEL kits are priced higher at $34.90, however the build quality of MEL kits is at least as good if not better than KiwiCo. The diversity and research backing both brands is great, however KiwiCo has more variety and generally caters to more crafty projects and a fewer pure STEM projects. MEL Science also offers Virtual Reality lessons and DIY projects to subscribers allowing you to keep exploring beyond each individual kit.

 

KiwiCo VS WeCraftBox: WeCraftBox offers more arts and crafts style boxes rather than science and learning boxes, geared towards kids ages 3 to 9. WeCraftBox costs $29.99 per month compared to KiwiCo at $22.95. Both kits come with everything you need in the box. If you want more variety in both content and age ranges, and if learning is your priority, then KiwiCo is probably a better choice, however if you just want to get creative and hands-on then WeCraftBox gives you lots of activities to keep you occupied.
description:
KiwiCo is a monthly subscription box that inspires kids to see themselves as makers, engineering and creating their own innovative designs and outcomes. They offer multiple lines of fun and enriching STEAM projects that are designed to spark creativity, tinkering, innovation and learning. Ultimately, KiwiCo's mission is to provide the next generation of innovators with the tools and foundation to become creative problem-solvers and critical thinkers. Crates range from the Panda Crate designed for babies up to the Eureka Crate that features engineering projects for ages 12 and up. KiwiCo is so popular it was voted as the best subscription box for kids in the 2021 readers’ choice awards.

Here are the crates offered by Kiwico:

Panda Crate for 0-24-month-olds,

Koala Crate for 2-4-year-olds,

Kiwi Crate for 5-8-year-olds, 

Yummy Crate for 6-14,

Atlas Crate for 6-11-year-olds,

Doodle Crate and Tinker Crate for 9-16+-year-olds,

Eureka Crate and Maker Crate for ages 14 and up.

name: Maker Crate by KiwiCo
heading: Maker Crate by KiwiCo Reviews
subheading: Is Maker Crate by KiwiCo Right For You?
description: Maker Crate is a monthly craft box dedicated to the discovery of new art and design tools to help turn artistic visions into design realities. Whether you're a new crafter or an experienced maker, each crate is designed to introduce fresh techniques, draw inspiration from real designs, and take pride in a finished project that's both fun and functional.Maker Crate is a monthly craft box dedicated to the discovery of new art and design tools to help turn artistic visions into design realities. Whether you're a new crafter or an experienced maker, each crate is designed to introduce fresh techniques, draw inspiration from real designs, and take pride in a finished project that's both fun and functional.

name: Monthly Jewelry Tree
heading: Monthly Jewelry Tree Reviews
subheading: Is Monthly Jewelry Tree Right For You?
description: "Every month, you will receive a piece of jewelry AND a gift bag and tag to pass it along to a friend if it’s not your style. Be the base of your very own jewelry tree. Items will vary in style and type (bracelets, watches, necklaces, earrings etc) and if you order more than one, you will receive different items and multiple gift bags."

name: Once Upon A Book Club
heading: Once Upon A Book Club Reviews
subheading: Is Once Upon A Book Club Right For You?
description: Bringing Books to Life. Monthly! Each month, you'll receive a newly-released Adult or Young Adult novel to read, along with 3-5 wrapped gifts labeled with page numbers! As you read the featured novel, you'll find "Open Your Gift" stickers strategically placed inside the book to remind you to open the corresponding item! Each item is described within the novel and brought to life at the exact moment it means the most. Discover and connect with your favorite characters like never before!

name: OwlCrate

subheading: Is OwlCrate Right For You?
description: OwlCrate is a monthly subscription box for people who love Young Adult literature. Every month they send you a highly anticipated new YA release, as well as 3-5 bookish items (such as fandom jewelry, collectible toys, bookmarks, and more!). Each box will also include exclusive content from authors and publishers too! they ship anywhere in the world, with different shipping costs for different areas.

name: Penny + Grace
heading: Penny + Grace Reviews
subheading: Is Penny + Grace Right For You?
description: Each month, Penny + Grace subscribers receive 3 delicate jewelry pieces in the metal of their choice: 18k Gold or Silver. All jewelry is 100% Lead and Nickel Free, and subscribers can choose whether or not to include earrings. Each box has a retail value of $100+.

name: Stitch Fix
heading: Stitch Fix Reviews
subheading: Is Stitch Fix Right For You?
content:
What It’s Like to Subscribe to Stitch Fix

 

Read reviews further down the page, too, for more personal experiences.

How Stitch Fix Works

When you first visit stitch fix, the first thing you do is take a style quiz. The quiz takes a few minutes but helps Stitch Fix learn what your preferences are so their style experts and algorithms can recommend more clothes you love and fewer clothes you aren’t interested in. Questions include things like shopping preferences, clothing style and type preferences, body proportions, and price ranges.

After completing an order, your first fix is then shipped. You can try on and see which clothes you want to keep, if any, and which clothes you want to return. There are no shipping charges for either deliveries or returns. You only pay for two things: $20 for the styling service, and the price of any clothes you choose to keep. The $20 styling service is applied as a discount towards any clothes you keep, too! If you do end up keeping your whole fix, then you’ll also receive a 25% discount towards the whole batch.

Stitch fix no longer requires a subscription, so you can get fixes shipped individually or on a recurring basis, and if you want clothes for multiple people including men, women, kids & more, you can specify different frequencies for each.

Example Stitch Fix Styles and Pricing Options

Pricing for Stitch Fix is always the same: $20 for every fix shipped as a styling fee, plus the cost of any clothes you keep, with the $20 styling fee applied as a direct discount towards the clothes. That means the minimum cost is always $20, with the maximum cost varying depending on the price of the clothes and what styles you receive.

Check out all of our Stitch Fix editorial reviews to see what styles our expert reviewers received in their fixes and what they cost.

Stitch Fix Pros and Cons

Pros

  • Quickly adapts to your style preferences
  • Styles are accessible to most people
  • Very convenient
  • High quality clothes
  • Tons of brands carried

Cons

  • Less affordable than bargain hunting in-store
  • Short 3-day return window if you don’t request an extension
  • When boxes do miss the mark, it can be frustrating or discouraging

What Else You Should Know About Stitch Fix

 

The three-day return period seems short, but you aren’t stuck with it - you can request an extension through your profile which can give you that extra time you need to decide if the clothes are really right for you.

One of the main strengths of Stitch Fix is their algorithm to find the right clothes for you from the thousands of options out there. To improve the usefulness of the algorithm, you can also play Stitch Fix’s “style shuffle”, a version of swiping left and right like Tinder but for clothes. By quickly sharing what you do or don’t like, the algorithm can become more refined faster than waiting weeks to learn about only the handful of pieces actually delivered.

You can also try sharing Pinterest boards full of images of clothes you like (or don’t like) to help your stylist pick the right clothes for you. That said, we’ve heard mixed results with this tactic since not all stylists are the same and some stylists rely more or less on the algorithmic suggestions as opposed to using your feedback (for better or for worse).

One more note on affordability - while Stitch Fix defaults to selling clothes at MSRP, if you do find an item cheaper within seven days of delivery then Stitch Fix has a price-matching policy. This only applies to sticker price, though, and doesn’t apply to sales.

Is Stitch Fix Worth It?

 

For most people looking to expand their wardrobe from the convenience of home, Stitch Fix is a great option that has a good chance of delivering you new pieces that fit your style.

If you are someone who loves the in-store experience, seeks more adventurous styles, or if you’re a bargain hunter who wants to look for the best deals, then Stitch Fix is probably not for you.

How Stitch Fix Compares to Other Top Styling Services

 

Stitch Fix VS Trunk Club: Both of these styling services try to match your preferences with clothes you’ll love, shipped to your door for you to try on and either keep or return. Stitch Fix tends to be a little more conservative in their style suggestions and more affordable. The $25 styling fee Trunk Club charges is only $5 more, but the price of each clothing item is usually much higher. With Trunk Club, however, you have the Nordstrom name behind it, which brings the in-store option and top tier customer service to go with the higher end items.

Stitch Fix VS Wantable: These styling services are very similar. They have the same $20 styling fee, but Wantable’s clothing options tend to be a little less expensive and catered towards more trendy styles. Stitch Fix carries far more brands and caters to more types of styles overall, however. Wantable only has a 20% discount if you keep 5 items, however they ship 7 items instead of only 5 so you have a better shot at the discount. Wantable also gives you five days to return compared to Stitch Fix’s three. Wantable has a “stream” where you can specifically request clothes or decline clothes, influencing what might show up in your next shipment. Wantable Fitness is a better option than Stitch Fix if you’re looking for activewear.
description: Stitch Fix is an online personal styling service for women, men, and kids too! You tell them all about your style and size preferences through their style quiz, and then they send you a package of carefully selected stylish goodies to try on at home. No two Fix shipments are alike and each one is hand-picked just for you. Keep what items you like, and return the rest in a pre-paid return mailer - no subscription required. The service fee is $20 a month for your stylist, but that fee goes towards any items you choose to keep as a discount. Stitch Fix also offers an expanded size range including plus, petites, and maternity.   Stitch Fix is so popular with MSA readers, they voted it onto every one of our fashion and clothing reader’s choice lists! It ranked #1 on best men’s clothing and style subscriptions, and near the top for best women’s fashion subscriptions and best kids clothing subscription boxes too.

 Wantable offers four different Edits: Women's Style, Women's Active, Sleep & Body, and Men's Active.
 
 Each Active Edit features 7 expertly styled activewear and lounge items. All styled to fit your unique taste, shape and budget."

name: Wantable Style Edit

description: Wantable is a personal styling service that sends high-quality clothing and accessories based on a user's style profile after taking a style quiz. There is a $20 styling fee which is applied to any purchase made from your “Edit” and if you keep 5 or more items, you receive a 20% discount as well. Wantable offers three different styling subscription services: the Women's Style Edit, Fitness Edit, and Men’s Fitness Edit. Each styling service allows members to try out different styles at home before committing to their purchase. This means members can try 7 clothing and/or accessory items handpicked just for them in the comfort of home before buying them. Brands offered by Wantable include Project Social, BB Dakota, W. by Wantable, Cupcakes & Cashmere, Kut from the Kloth, Simply Threads – and more. Items prices vary, with a typical range of $50 to $100 each, and thresholds are set by the subscriber. While the Edits are a monthly subscription, members can easily skip a month or cancel at any time.

With a themed story, 4-5 coordinating crafts, easy-to-follow photo directions and video tutorials, and all materials included, that can be shared between 2 children, we’re helping busy families unplug from the screen and connect through creativity!

name: Winc
heading: Winc Reviews
subheading: Is Winc Right For You?
content:
What It’s Like to Subscribe to Winc

 

Read reviews further down the page, too, for more personal experiences.

How Winc Works

Joining Winc is easy. When you subscribe through MSA your first box comes with a major discount. First fill out their short palette quiz and then Winc will pair you with four wines that their sommeliers have curated to fit your preferences. You’re not limited just to their recommendations - if you explore the shop, you can replace or add any bottles you find to your box. If you want more than four bottles you can keep adding, and if you order 12 or more you get an extra 10% off.

Your first box comes with an introduction to wines that helps you learn the vocab of the wine world - acidity, oxidation, tannins - if you want to learn, Winc is there for you. But you don’t need to know much, most wines have easy to follow descriptions and you just need to tell winc how you would rate each bottle after you’ve tasted it. Winc will take your feedback and keep recommending you better wines over time.

Your membership renews each month where you will be charged and receive credits towards your next box. Winc recommends you wines every month but as with your first box you can always customize your wines by keeping, replacing, or adding to their recommendations.

Example Winc Wine Bottles

The exact wines available in the Winc store will rotate continually throughout the year, but to give you an example of the range here are some wines we saw in the store at Members only prices:

  • 2019 Wonderful Wine Co. White Blend for $16.99
  • 2020 Pacificana Chardonnay for $12.99
  • 2018 Fog Land Merlot for $14.99
  • 2020 Summer Water Rosé for $17.99
  • 2019 Le Fermier Cabernet Sauvignon for $18.99
  • House of Luck Saké for $24.99
  • 2020 Baseline Chardonnay for $34.99

Winc features

Frequency Monthly
Membership Price $59.99 after your first month
Members Cost per Bottle $12.99 - $39.99
Vegan Options? Yes
Regions California, France, Italy, Argentina, Spain, Portugal
Bulk Discount 10% orders of 12+ bottles
A La Carte Shopping Yes
Shipping Cost FREE for 4+ bottles, otherwise $9

Winc Pros and Cons

Pros

  • Wide range of wines to explore with clear tasting notes
  • You don’t have to be an expert to find wines you’ll enjoy
  • Recommendations become more accurate after you rate a few wines
  • More wines being added all the time

Cons

  • Your favorite might not be around forever
  • If you don’t drink at least four bottles a month, credits can accumulate
  • A la carte shopping is not working due to site malfunctions
  • While most people have good experiences, if you need support then service levels have decreased lately

What Else You Should Know About Winc

 

Shipping wine can be convenient but it could also introduce headaches if you don’t plan ahead. Make sure someone 21 or over is there to sign for it - in most places they won’t leave alcohol on your doorstep if no one is home, and in the winter you wouldn’t want them to in case the wine freezes and explodes in the box.

One good option is to have your wine shipped either to your place of work or to a pick up location where someone will be there to receive it and you can go pick it up at your convenience.

Is Winc Worth It?

 

After reviewing Winc numerous times and reviewing subscriber ratings, we can confidently say that a Winc membership is worth it. The recommendations keep the quality high so you can explore with less risk and the price per bottle is worth it for the quality, guides, and convenience.

If you are not a frequent wine drinker or your alcohol budget generally is not at least $60 per month already, then it may be better to stick with a la carte purchases rather than a subscription, unless you are hoping to expand your wine collection or try more wines in the future.

How Winc Compares to Other Top Wine Subscriptions

 

Winc VS Firstleaf: These wine subscriptions are very similar - both have you take a quiz, match wines to your preferences, have connoisseur curations, and ship monthly. You can customize your box for both brands and you can get credit for bottles that miss the mark. Firstleaf ships six bottles standard compared to Winc’s four. Winc is a bit more affordable with most bottles priced at $12.99 compared to Firstleaf’s $15 price, however Firstleaf focuses more on delivering past award-winning wines rather than exploring new wines.

 

Winc VS Bright Cellars: Bright Cellars is another similar wine subscription that uses a quiz and tasting notes to pair you with wines you might love, with a delight guarantee to ship a replacement bottle if you don’t like something you received. Bright Cellars is more global than Winc and the price reflects that at $20 per bottle compared to $12.99 at Winc.

 

Winc VS Vine Oh!: Vine Oh is a much more casual wine experience designed for women that focuses much less on personalization and much more on the fun side of drinking. It is priced similarly to Winc but your only customization is whether you want Reds, Whites, Both, or sweet wines.
description: Winc is a wine subscription based in California that uses sommelier tasting notes and curations alongside your own personal taste preferences to recommend the perfect wines for you. It’s that simple! Members receive discounts on wine, shipping, and gifting, plus enjoy regular promotions and a referral program. Winc is popular with MSA readers and was voted as the best wine subscription box of 2021!
